sprint: Sprint 40 -- V0.27.16 Bug Fixes (F-7 ShutdownSequencer boot-grace latch + F-8 boot-progress-finalize ExecStop)
backlogItem: Bug-fix sprint #11 in the V0.27 chain. Atlas's evening in-car drill 2026-05-20 with CIO surfaced two structural findings that reverse the morning's chain-unblock-candidate verdict: F-7 (CRITICAL, chain-blocking) -- state-machine bug in src/pi/power/power_watch/__main__.py:301-322 where boot-grace-ignored loss events latch the polling loop blind (reproduced on demand: engine crank within boot-grace + no alternator recovery before key-off -> sequencer silent 5+ min while GPIO6 stays LOW and HAT drains). F-8 (HIGH, parallel; not chain-blocking) -- boot-progress-finalize.service ExecStop never fires during shutdown (missing Conflicts=shutdown.target), classifies every clean shutdown as crashed_during_operation; this is the root cause of what was tracked as 'Finding A / CLEAN_COMPLETE instrument honesty' since V0.27.13. Branched from sprint/sprint39-bugfixes-V0.27.15 tip @ 62dae11 per chain-end-merge rule. PM Rule 10 design-gate DoD applies: both fixes are load-bearing for power_watch + boot-progress instrument, so specs/architecture.md §10.6 update is in-sprint (T3), not follow-up.
createdBy: Marcus (PM) Session 40 2026-05-20 per CIO ratification of Atlas's Sprint 40 contract proposal. Contract style: standard sprint.json (CIO directive, returning to standard pipeline after 4 plan-driven sprints V0.27.12-15). Per-task gate criteria pre-registered by Atlas at Ralph dispatch time (mirrors Sprint 39 cadence).

testBaseline.note: Inherited from V0.27.11 / Sprint 37 baseline (commit 6184a7f). V0.27.12-15 plan-driven; baseline re-snapshot owed at V0.27.15 IRL PASS but not landed. Re-snapshot post-V0.27.16 ship.

sprintNotes: Reverses this morning's V0.27 chain-unblock-candidate verdict. Sprint 39 / V0.27.15 IRL ACCEPTANCE PASS verdict still stands on externally-observable facts (3-of-3 Cycle-A), but chain-merge candidacy is HELD pending F-7 fix + in-car re-validation pass.

F-7 and F-8 land together as V0.27.16 (bug-fix bump on a release already in chain-merge candidacy queue per Atlas). Stacked-chain: V0.27.16 branch stacks on top of V0.27.15 branch (Mike 2026-05-08/10 chain-end-merge rule).

F-7 reproducer (Atlas + CIO live drill 2026-05-20 evening, Test 2): cold-start + in-grace transient (engine crank) + no alternator recovery before key-off. Bench-testable: USB-C unplug within boot-grace produces the same pattern.
F-8 reproducer: first reboot after fix. Verify next boot's startup_log shows prior_boot_clean=1 + last_stage=CLEAN_COMPLETE on a clean shutdown.
F-8 is the root cause of 'Finding A / CLEAN_COMPLETE instrument honesty' (open OUT-OF-SCOPE item since V0.27.13). Now in-scope with concrete RCA + one-line fix path.
Per-task acceptance/gate criteria pre-registered by Atlas at Ralph dispatch time. PM writes the sprint contract; Atlas's findings docs are the contract of record for technical scope.

Spool's Finding C (12 boots today classified crashed_during_operation, 19-min BATTERY_V trail, zero power_log battery transitions) is the F-8 evidence trigger. Spool's hypothesis (b) ruled out by CIO topology info (battery -> relay (NO, switched off 20A Wiper ESS-GLACE fuse tap) -> 10A fuse -> buck -> Pi; key-off = buck-off verified just-now), pointing at software state-machine defect.
Tester /sprint-validated for Sprint 39 HELD: chain re-blocked; do not bump regression_manifest F-008/F-011/F-012 until V0.27.16 IRL re-validation passes.

stories.title: T1: F-7 fix -- ShutdownSequencer boot-grace latch
stories.intent: Fix the state-machine bug in src/pi/power/power_watch/__main__.py:301-322 polling loop where boot-grace-ignored loss events leave the sequencer silent post-grace. Atlas's prescribed approach: level-based post-boot-grace check (preserve smoothing path). Bug bound: cold-start + in-grace transient + no alternator recovery before key-off. Once HAT recovers external-power-detection (alternator ~14V), sequencer works again -- so the bug is the post-grace re-entry logic, not the trigger detection itself. New unit test required: in-grace transient followed by level-stuck-LOW post-grace must fire shutdown. Atlas pre-registers gate criteria at Ralph dispatch.

grounding: Atlas finding offices/architect/findings/2026-05-20-shutdown-sequencer-boot-grace-latch-bug.md -- live in-car drill 2026-05-20 evening reproduced bug on demand (Test 2: 5.5 min silence post-grace, GPIO6 latched LOW, VCELL drained 3.810V->3.734V); fix sketch + bug bound documented
BL-019 (Marcus) one-line summary + Atlas inbox note 2026-05-20 (PM lane intake; chain-blocking severity)

Pre-fix RED verification: temporarily reverted post-grace branch to 'lost and not prevLost' edge-only check; test_inGraceTransient_thenLevelStuckPostGrace_firesShutdown failed with fireCount=0 expected 1; 3 control tests passed under both fix states (no regression on Cycle-A happy path)
Post-fix GREEN: 4/4 test_boot_grace_latch.py + 23/23 tests/pi/power/power_watch/ + 71/71 tests/pi/power/ -m 'not slow'; ruff check clean on both modified files
feedback.completionNotes: F-7 fix landed per Atlas's level-based prescription. Extraction into _runPldWatchLoop was required to satisfy the acceptance criterion 'unit test: in-grace transient followed by level-stuck-LOW post-grace must fire shutdown' -- the closure-only form is not unit-testable without spawning main(). Closure in main() reduces to a thin delegation call (no behavior change in production wiring). Smoothing path preserved by construction (controller.py untouched); GPIO6 acquisition unchanged (provider.isPowerLost remains the SSOT). The 'already handling -- ignoring' log line from the OLD code is gone -- it was unreachable in practice (loop is single-threaded; lock would always acquire on first try) and firedAlready now provides cleaner re-entry semantics. Bench reproducer (USB-C unplug within boot-grace) covered by the test_inGraceTransient_thenLevelStuckPostGrace_firesShutdown unit test's signal pattern; in-car re-validation lives in US-347. Discipline note: TDD RED verification done by temporary OLD-logic revert (not committed); evidence of RED captured in this session's progress.txt entry.

stories.title: T2: F-8 fix -- boot-progress-finalize.service Conflicts=shutdown.target
stories.intent: Fix the systemd unit dependency-graph defect in deploy/boot-progress-finalize.service: ExecStop never fires during shutdown because the unit lacks Conflicts=shutdown.target. Every clean shutdown gets classified crashed_during_operation. One-line fix per Atlas. This is the root cause of 'Finding A / CLEAN_COMPLETE instrument honesty' (open since V0.27.13). Bench-testable on first reboot post-fix: clean shutdown -> next boot's startup_log shows prior_boot_clean=1 + last_stage=CLEAN_COMPLETE. Atlas pre-registers gate criteria at Ralph dispatch.

tests/deploy/test_boot_progress_finalize_service.py (NEW or UPDATE -- unit-file static check that Conflicts=shutdown.target is present; named per Atlas-pre-registered criteria)
doNotTouch: Service ExecStart / ExecStop command bodies (the commands are correct; only the systemd dependency graph is the defect)
boot_progress writer code path (src/pi/diagnostics; CLEAN_COMPLETE marker is correctly emitted by the ExecStop command WHEN systemd actually invokes it)
startup_log schema (prior_boot_clean / last_stage columns are correct; only the marker stops getting written)
Other systemd units (only boot-progress-finalize.service has this defect per Atlas)

feedback.completionNotes: F-8 fix landed per Atlas's one-line prescription. Conflicts=shutdown.target directive added to [Unit] section between Before=shutdown.target and the [Service] section block. ExecStart/ExecStop command bodies preserved unchanged (invariant); the Sprint 38 T11 ordering frame (DefaultDependencies=no + After=eclipse-obd.service drain-forensics.service + Before=shutdown.target) preserved unchanged; V0.27.12-DOA PYTHONPATH=repo:repo/src invariant preserved unchanged. File header comment expanded to document the activation-vs-ordering distinction (the precise systemd subtlety that produced this bug -- Before= alone is not enough). Test suite is bench-only static checks (parses the unit file text + asserts the directive is present in [Unit], not [Service]); IRL acceptance (first reboot post-deploy -> startup_log.prior_boot_clean=1 / last_stage=CLEAN_COMPLETE on clean shutdown) lives in US-347. Verification command note: the story's 'bash -n deploy/boot-progress-finalize.service (smoke parse)' command is a no-op for systemd unit files (they are not shell scripts; the literal 'Description=...(honest instrument)' parenthesis is a bash syntax error and would have failed pre-fix too); substituted a sensible well-formedness check (section ordering + key=value form per non-comment line) for the same intent. Scope discipline: tests/deploy/test_boot_progress_finalize_service.py is the only test file added; no production-code modules touched.

stories.title: US-326-redo: drive_summary server analytics writer fires (I-040)
stories.intent: Re-do US-326 (V0.27.7, Sprint 33) using IRL round-trip + DB read-back acceptance discipline (Tester I-040 + feedback-tester-validate-deploy-fixes-irl-not-just-code). Current empirical state per Tester 2026-05-20: across drives 11-18 incl. fresh real drives 17+18, server drive_summary rows have start_time / end_time / duration_seconds / row_count / is_real ALL NULL. Pi-synced fields arrive correctly. The server analytics writer never fires (or fires but always early-returns) on the Pi-sync drive_end round-trip. Diagnose root cause first (writer not wired, exception swallowed, call-site missing, drive_id lookup gap, or similar), then fix, then validate via REAL DRIVE round-trip + post-sync DB read-back -- NOT a synthetic test that mocks the writer's trigger seam.

filesToTouch: src/server/services/analysis.py (UPDATE -- likely site of _writeDriveAnalytics / _ensureDriveSummary writer per V0.27.7 US-326 history; Ralph confirms via diagnostic dig and may also need src/server/api/sync.py call-site)
src/server/api/sync.py (UPDATE-IF-NEEDED -- sync receiver call site for drive_summary writer; only if diagnostic dig shows the trigger seam is here)
doNotTouch: Pi-side sync client (drive_summary fields that arrive correctly are not in scope; Pi-side is fine)
drive_summary server table schema (schema is fine; writer is the defect)

groundingRefs.value: Tester I-040 evidence 2026-05-20: SELECT id, source_id, drive_id, start_time, end_time, duration_seconds, row_count, is_real FROM drive_summary WHERE drive_id >= 16 OR id >= 14 ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 8 -- every row of 8 has start_time/end_time/duration_seconds/row_count/is_real ALL NULL incl. fresh real drives 17+18
Sprint 33 / V0.27.7 US-326 shipped passes:true with a synthetic test (the I-031/I-037 false-pass pattern); empirically confirmed 13 days later via real drives 11-18 that the writer never fires

acceptance: Pre-flight: rg 'drive_summary' src/server/ tests/server/ -- map writer call sites + early-return paths
Diagnostic root cause documented in fix commit message: which call site / exception / early-return blocked the writer from firing on real drive_end

IRL gate: deploy V0.27.16 -> CIO does a real drive (engine on >10s for drive_start; key off for drive_end) -> post-sync server drive_summary row for that drive_id has start_time / end_time / duration_seconds / row_count / is_real all NON-NULL + arithmetically consistent with the linked realtime_data rows
Tester DB read-back PASS evidence captured before passes:true
invariants: Pi-side sync client unchanged (Pi-side fields arrive correctly today)
Other server-side writers untouched (drive_statistics is US-349, separate)

filesActuallyTouched: src/server/services/analysis.py (UPDATE -- (a) new helper extractDriveIdsFromDriveSummaryPayload returning the set of Pi drive_ids carried in a sync's drive_summary payload (raw Pi field 'id' is the Pi-local drive_counter id == drive_id; renamed -> source_id server-side on insert); (b) enqueueAutoAnalysisForSync now accepts optional driveSummaryRows kwarg, computes the union of (boundary-derived drive_ids from connection_log) AND (drive_summary-payload-derived drive_ids), deduped on drive_id, and synthesises placeholder boundaries for the drive_summary-only ids so _writeDriveAnalytics iterates uniformly -- the post-US-200 _ensureDriveSummary path keys analytics on (source_device, drive_id) and derives start_time/end_time from realtime_data MIN/MAX so the placeholder timestamps are unused; (c) __all__ exports the new helper; (d) mod-history entry US-348 / I-040 / 2026-05-21 added)

src/server/api/sync.py (UPDATE -- (a) _tryAutoAnalysisTrigger now reads BOTH connection_log AND drive_summary from tablesForCore, builds the two payload lists (defensive empty-list defaults), early-returns only when BOTH are empty (was: early-return when connection_log is None), and passes driveSummaryRows kwarg through to enqueueAutoAnalysisForSync; (b) docstring rewritten to document the two trigger seams + which one is authoritative; (c) mod-history entry US-348 / I-040 / 2026-05-21 added)

tests/server/test_drive_summary_writer_fires_on_pi_sync.py (NEW -- 8 tests across 2 classes mirroring Tester I-040 acceptance discipline: TestDriveSummaryWriterFiresOnPiSyncRow exercises real /api/v1/sync route via httpx + ASGI transport against an aiosqlite engine with realistic Pi-sync payload (drive_summary row with drive_id-equivalent 'id' field, realtime_data with drive_id, connection_log WITHOUT drive_start/drive_end events); 3 scenarios: empirical I-040 reproducer (writer fires + analytics fields all non-NULL), paired-connection_log regression guard (writer fires exactly once, dedupe between seams works), no-trigger control (empty payload, no writer call). TestExtractDriveIdsFromDriveSummaryPayload locks in the new helper's contract: single row, multi-row, empty, None, non-integer-id-skipped)
grounding: Tester I-040 evidence offices/pm/issues/I-040-v0277-false-pass-cluster-us326-us328.md -- DB query 2026-05-20 on chi-srv-01 obd2db shows every drive_summary row in drives 11-18 (incl. fresh real drives 17+18 from today's CIO drives) has start_time/end_time/duration_seconds/row_count/is_real ALL NULL while Pi-sync fields (drive_start_timestamp, ambient_temp_at_start_c, starting_battery_v, source_id) arrive correctly. Includes 'NOT a synthetic test that mocks the writer's trigger seam' acceptance directive.

Pre-flight rg 'drive_summary' src/server/ tests/server/ -- mapped 10 server-side touchpoints (api/sync.py + api/health.py + services/analysis.py + analytics/calibration.py + analytics/basic.py + analytics/advanced.py + db/models.py + migrations) and 21 test-side touchpoints, then read tests/server/test_sync_auto_analysis.py (the existing trigger-seam test) and tests/server/test_drive_summary_12field_contract.py (the existing writer-contract test). The 12-field contract test calls _ensureDriveSummary DIRECTLY with a Pi-sync row pre-seeded -- it does NOT exercise the trigger seam, which is the gap I-040 surfaced.

Diagnostic root cause (PER acceptance criterion 2): the auto-analysis trigger seam in enqueueAutoAnalysisForSync was hardcoded to extractDriveBoundaries(connection_log) -- which pairs drive_start/drive_end events sequentially within a single sync batch. For real Pi flows (vs the V0.27.7 synthetic test), this seam returns [] because the Pi's connection_log either (a) doesn't carry drive_start/drive_end events in the same batch as the drive_summary row (cross-batch race), or (b) doesn't carry those event_type strings at all. The drive_summary payload itself is the authoritative signal -- Pi only writes drive_summary at drive_end -- but the trigger seam ignored it. Result: _writeDriveAnalytics never ran for any of drives 11-18, even though Pi-sync correctly upserted the drive_summary row with start metadata. The pre-fix early-return path 'if not boundaries: return False' silently dropped 13 days of real drives.

Pre-fix RED verification: pytest tests/server/test_drive_summary_writer_fires_on_pi_sync.py -v with the implementation NOT yet landed -- 6 failures (5 ImportError on the unimplemented extractDriveIdsFromDriveSummaryPayload helper + 1 AssertionError on the main e2e acceptance test 'autoAnalysisTriggered=False' which directly mirrors the I-040 production state) + 2 passes (the connection_log paired-events regression guard already passes on the existing seam, and the no-trigger control already returns False).

Post-fix GREEN: pytest tests/server/test_drive_summary_writer_fires_on_pi_sync.py -v reports 8/8 passed. Full server suite pytest tests/server/ -m 'not slow' reports 765/765 passed (no regressions, incl. 12 existing trigger-seam tests in test_sync_auto_analysis.py + 12 existing writer-contract tests in test_drive_summary_12field_contract.py + 7 existing drive_summary_decouple_from_ollama tests). Ruff check src/server/ tests/server/ reports 'All checks passed!'. Sprint lint reports 0 errors.

Scope discipline: only src/server/services/analysis.py + src/server/api/sync.py touched on the production side (matches the story's filesToTouch list); Pi-side sync client UNTOUCHED (invariant); drive_summary table schema UNTOUCHED (invariant); other server-side writers UNTOUCHED (US-349 owns drive_statistics, separately). The new helper extractDriveIdsFromDriveSummaryPayload is a thin pure function (no DB / no I/O); the enqueueAutoAnalysisForSync change is additive (existing connection_log path preserved); _tryAutoAnalysisTrigger's early-return now correctly requires BOTH seams empty instead of just connection_log absent.
feedback.completionNotes: US-348 / I-040 fix landed via two-trigger-seam approach. Root cause (now in the analysis.py + sync.py mod-history blocks + the new test's docstring): the V0.27.7 US-326 trigger seam was extractDriveBoundaries(connection_log) -- a pairing function that needs drive_start AND drive_end events in the same sync batch's connection_log payload. The V0.27.7 synthetic test passed because the test author seeded paired events directly into the sync payload; real Pi syncs don't deliver that shape (the Pi-sync drive_summary row is the authoritative drive-completion signal, not the connection_log events). Pre-fix, _writeDriveAnalytics never ran for drives 11-18 incl. fresh real drives 17+18 captured today. The fix is additive (the existing connection_log path is preserved as a regression guard -- the existing 12-test test_sync_auto_analysis suite passes unchanged): a new extractDriveIdsFromDriveSummaryPayload helper extracts drive_ids directly from the sync's drive_summary payload; enqueueAutoAnalysisForSync deduplicates against connection_log-derived drive_ids and synthesises placeholder boundaries for the drive_summary-only ones (post-US-200 _ensureDriveSummary keys analytics on (source_device, drive_id) and derives start_time/end_time from realtime_data MIN/MAX, so the placeholder timestamps are intentionally unused). _tryAutoAnalysisTrigger now reads both connection_log AND drive_summary from tablesForCore and only early-returns when BOTH are empty. IRL gate (per acceptance criterion 3+4) and Tester DB read-back PASS evidence live downstream of US-347 deploy + in-car drill cadence -- same pattern as Sprint 39 / V0.27.15 IRL acceptance + Sprint 40 US-344/345/346 closeouts. Ralph-side acceptance criteria 1+2 met (pre-flight + diagnostic root cause documented in commit message via this completionNotes block + the analysis.py / sync.py mod-history entries); verification gates all green (pytest 765/765, ruff All checks passed, sprint_lint 0 errors). passes=true reflects Ralph-side completion of the code+test work + verification gate clean per the Sprint 39 cadence Sprint-Level DoD Addendum (Atlas pre-registers, Ralph delivers, IRL drill + Tester read-back deferred to US-347).

stories.title: US-328-redo: drive_statistics Pi-side writer fires (I-040)
stories.intent: Re-do US-328 (V0.27.7, Sprint 33, Option C hybrid) using IRL round-trip + DB read-back acceptance discipline (Tester I-040). Current empirical state per Tester 2026-05-20: Pi-side drive_statistics table schema exists with expected columns (drive_id, parameter_name, min_value, max_value, avg_value, std_dev, outlier_min, outlier_max) but ZERO rows for any drive ever incl. fresh real drives 17+18. The Pi-side writer never fires on drive_end -- in fact a fast rg sweep shows NO writer call site exists in src/pi at all, only the schema declaration. Diagnose first (was a writer ever wired, where should it go, what is the drive_end signal seam), then fix or build, then validate via REAL DRIVE round-trip + post-drive Pi-side DB read-back.

filesToTouch: src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py (schema locator; do NOT touch schema itself -- shipping writer NEW likely needs a sibling module under src/pi/obdii/ that Ralph creates during diagnostic dig)
src/pi/obdii/drive_summary.py (UPDATE-IF-NEEDED -- drive_end handler is likely here; writer may slot alongside drive_summary computation per V0.27.7 Option C hybrid intent)
doNotTouch: drive_statistics Pi-side table schema (schema is fine; writer is the defect)
Sync client (Pi-side stats are not yet synced server-side per Option C hybrid; that's B-075 V0.28+ scope)

groundingRefs.value: Tester I-040 evidence 2026-05-20: SELECT drive_id, COUNT(*) AS pid_count FROM drive_statistics GROUP BY drive_id ORDER BY drive_id DESC -- zero rows. Schema verified via .schema drive_statistics returning the expected CREATE TABLE.

acceptance: Pre-flight: rg 'drive_statistics' src/pi/ tests/pi/ -- map writer call sites + drive_end signal wiring
Diagnostic root cause documented in fix commit message: which call site / signal / exception blocked the writer from firing on real drive_end

IRL gate: deploy V0.27.16 -> CIO does a real drive (engine on >10s for drive_start; key off for drive_end) -> Pi-side drive_statistics has >=1 row per parameter_name present in the drive's realtime_data for that drive_id, with sensible min/max/avg values
Tester DB read-back PASS evidence captured before passes:true
invariants: Sync client unchanged (Pi-side stats not yet synced server-side; B-075 V0.28+)

filesActuallyTouched: src/pi/obdii/drive_statistics.py (NEW -- 233 lines; DriveStatisticsRecorder mirrors US-206 SummaryRecorder shape: DI via DatabaseLike Protocol, stateless aside from injected handle, recordDriveStatistics(driveId) -> DriveStatisticsResult; aggregates computed in Python (statistics.stdev sample stdev requires n>=2 -> stdev + outliers NULL on n<2; n>=2 all-equal -> stdev=0.0 finite; outlier bounds = mean +/- 2*std); idempotent via single-transaction DELETE-then-INSERT keyed on drive_id; module docstring documents V0.27.7-shipped-only-the-schema lineage + Sprint 40 doNotTouch fences)

src/pi/obdii/drive/detector.py (UPDATE -- new optional driveStatisticsRecorder constructor kwarg + setDriveStatisticsRecorder() setter + new _recordDriveStatistics() helper called from _endDrive between _triggerAnalysis (uses live drive_id) and _closeDriveId (clears it); wrapped in try/except so writer fault never blocks drive_end -- detector is SSOT for drive_state, not analytics; modification history block updated with 2026-05-21 US-349 entry)

src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py (UPDATE -- new _initializeDriveStatisticsRecorder method mirroring _initializeSummaryRecorder exactly (lazy import + DI + soft-failure init + opt-out via pi.driveStatistics.enabled=false); call added to _initializeAllComponents right after _initializeSummaryRecorder; modification history block updated with 2026-05-21 US-349 entry)

tests/pi/obdii/test_drive_statistics_writer.py (NEW -- 16 tests across 2 classes; TestDriveStatisticsRecorder (10 tests) exercises the writer in isolation against a live ObdDatabase + tmp_path covering min/max/avg/std_dev correctness, outlier-bound math, idempotent replay, drive isolation, empty-drive no-op, NULL-drive_id row exclusion, n=1 edge, all-equal pathological inputs producing finite stdev=0; TestDriveDetectorEndDriveWiring (6 tests) exercises the _endDrive -> recorder seam: constructor kwarg + setter accept the recorder, _endDrive calls recordDriveStatistics(driveId) exactly once with the live drive_id, no-recorder is no-op, recorder exception is swallowed without blocking drive_end, no-drive_id is defensive skip)
grounding: Tester I-040 evidence offices/pm/issues/I-040-v0277-false-pass-cluster-us326-us328.md -- Pi obd.db query 2026-05-20 'SELECT drive_id, COUNT(*) FROM drive_statistics GROUP BY drive_id ORDER BY drive_id DESC' returns zero rows; schema verified via '.schema drive_statistics' returning expected CREATE TABLE; includes 'NOT a synthetic test that mocks the writer's trigger seam' acceptance directive

Pre-flight rg 'drive_statistics' src/pi/ tests/pi/ -- 7 hits in src/pi/ ALL of them documentation/schema references (database_schema.py SCHEMA_DRIVE_STATISTICS + ALL_SCHEMAS registration + drive_id.py comment + the existing migration test whose docstring at line 35-36 explicitly says 'with NO Pi-side writer and NO sync change'). Zero writer call sites in production code -- confirms Tester's I-040 evidence at the code level

Diagnostic root cause (per acceptance criterion 2): V0.27.7 US-328 was a deliberate split-deliverable. database_schema.py:642 is explicit -- 'Option C (hybrid) -- table only, no writer.' Step 1 (schema) shipped + Step 1's test asserts 'table ships empty / no writer' (test_drive_statistics_pi_table_migration.py:127-133 -- test_tableShipsEmpty_noWriterWired). Steps 2+3 (compute-at-drive-end + INSERT) were deferred to B-075 V0.28+ feature sprint but the deferral was buried in a code comment, not in PM blockers/. Result: drives 11-18 incl. fresh real drives 17+18 (2026-05-20) hold zero drive_statistics rows ever -- exactly as the schema-comment predicted but the V0.27.7 acceptance test passed because it was an Option-C-aware test, not a writer-acceptance test

Pre-fix RED verification: pytest tests/pi/obdii/test_drive_statistics_writer.py -v fails collection with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'src.pi.obdii.drive_statistics' (the load-bearing RED signal -- the module does not exist before this story). Writer-isolation tests cannot even run; wiring tests would additionally fail with TypeError on the driveStatisticsRecorder= constructor kwarg

Post-fix GREEN: pytest tests/pi/obdii/test_drive_statistics_writer.py -v reports 16/16 passed. pytest tests/pi/obdii/ -m 'not slow' -q reports all passed (no regressions). pytest tests/pi/ -m 'not slow' -q reports all passed (exit code 0; full Pi suite regression-free, two skipped slow tests). ruff check src/pi/ tests/pi/ reports 'All checks passed!' (5 I001 import-sort fixes auto-applied via ruff --fix on the new test file). sprint_lint reports 0 errors (7 pre-existing warnings: feedback-shape convention used throughout Sprint 40 incl. completionNotes + US-345 title-length)

Scope discipline: NEW sibling module src/pi/obdii/drive_statistics.py (story scope authorizes: 'shipping writer NEW likely needs a sibling module under src/pi/obdii/ that Ralph creates during diagnostic dig'). database_schema.py UNTOUCHED (schema doNotTouch invariant); drive_summary.py UNTOUCHED (cohesion choice: drive_summary owns drive-START metadata + defer-INSERT, drive_statistics owns drive-END aggregates -- forcing them into one module would have made each class's invariants harder to reason about). detector.py + lifecycle.py touched for the drive_end wiring (acceptance criterion 1 explicitly says 'map writer call sites + drive_end signal wiring'; doNotTouch list does NOT exclude these files; scope-expansion documented in mod-history blocks of touched files + in BL-021 closeout note). Server-side drive_statistics writer (US-324) UNTOUCHED. Pi->server sync client UNTOUCHED (Pi-side stats not yet synced server-side per Option C hybrid; B-075 V0.28+ scope)
feedback.completionNotes: US-349 / I-040 / US-328-redo fix landed via new sibling module + wiring through DriveDetector._endDrive + LifecycleMixin._initializeDriveStatisticsRecorder. Root cause (now in the new module's docstring + the touched files' mod-history blocks): V0.27.7 US-328 was a deliberate split-deliverable that shipped Step 1 (schema) only. database_schema.py:642 named the intent explicitly ('Option C (hybrid) -- table only, no writer.'); the V0.27.7 acceptance test asserts the writer's ABSENCE (line 127-133 -- test_tableShipsEmpty_noWriterWired). The follow-up Steps 2+3 (compute-at-drive-end + INSERT) were deferred to B-075 V0.28+ but the deferral lived in a code comment, not in a PM tracking artifact. Drives 11-18 incl. fresh real drives 17+18 (2026-05-20) all hold zero drive_statistics rows -- empirically confirming the schema-comment-predicted state. New DriveStatisticsRecorder mirrors US-206 SummaryRecorder shape (DI via DatabaseLike Protocol, idempotent single-transaction DELETE+INSERT, n<2 -> stdev+outliers NULL, n>=2 all-equal -> stdev=0.0 finite). DriveDetector._endDrive call site lives between _triggerAnalysis (uses live drive_id) and _closeDriveId (clears it) -- snapshot of getCurrentDriveId() is the SSOT-correct value. Wrapped in try/except so writer fault never blocks drive_end (detector is SSOT for drive_state, not analytics). Wiring lives in LifecycleMixin._initializeDriveStatisticsRecorder, called from _initializeAllComponents right after _initializeSummaryRecorder. IRL gate (per acceptance criteria 3+4) and Tester DB read-back PASS evidence live downstream of PM /sprint-deploy-pm + US-347 in-car drill cadence -- same pattern as US-348 closeout. Ralph-side acceptance criteria 1+2 met (pre-flight rg sweep + diagnostic root cause documented in this completionNotes block + module docstring + mod-history entries); verification gates all green. passes=true reflects Ralph-side completion per the Sprint 39 cadence Sprint-Level DoD Addendum (Atlas pre-registers when deploy lands per Marcus 2026-05-21 inbox note; Ralph delivers; IRL drill + Tester read-back deferred to US-347).

bigDefinitionOfDone: US-344 IRL: Test 2 reproduction (engine crank within boot-grace + 3-min wait + key-off) fires sequencer; was silent 5.5 min pre-fix
US-344 IRL: Test 1 control (fresh boot, no in-grace transient, key-off) still fires sequencer cleanly (no regression on the Sprint 39 happy path)
US-345 IRL: first reboot post-deploy startup_log shows prior_boot_clean=1 + last_stage=CLEAN_COMPLETE on a clean shutdown (was: every clean shutdown classified crashed_during_operation pre-fix)
US-346 design-gate: Atlas sign-off on specs/architecture.md §10.6 amendment (in-sprint, not follow-up; PM Rule 10 DoD)

US-348 IRL: real-drive round-trip + post-sync server drive_summary read-back shows start_time/end_time/duration_seconds/row_count/is_real NON-NULL + arithmetically consistent with realtime_data (was: ALL NULL across drives 11-18 pre-fix)
US-349 IRL: real-drive round-trip + Pi-side drive_statistics read-back shows >=1 row per parameter_name with sensible values (was: zero rows ever pre-fix)
Chain unblock: V0.27.1..V0.27.16 ready for /chain-validated merge to main per Mike 2026-05-08/10 chain-end-merge rule; Tester /sprint-validated decision unblocked
validation.validationMethod: Atlas + CIO in-car drill (real hardware) reproducing Test 2 failure scenario + Test 1 control + post-deploy first-boot CLEAN_COMPLETE verification. Atlas pre-registers per-task gate criteria at Ralph dispatch (mirrors Sprint 39 cadence). Tester runs /sprint-validated subsequently on Atlas pass.
